behavior_summary: U8P_GUARDEGG::hatch runs as a process to handle an egg reaching a ready-to-U8P_GUARDEGG::hatch state.
It checks the egg instance's id (Egg::getEggId(this)) and proceeds only when the id equals 0x01 (ready to U8P_GUARDEGG::hatch).
On U8P_GUARDEGG::hatch, it clears the egg state by calling Egg::setEggId(0x00, this).

If the global flag guard1met is not set, it sets the global flag xavierSitting = true and invokes Item::use as a separate process (argument passed by address from a local/stack slot), storing the result in a local variable 'temp'.
notable_control_flow: Entry as an engine process (processType 0x0210) for the implicit 'this' egg instance.
Primary conditional: proceed only when Egg::getEggId(this) == 0x01.
Always calls Egg::setEggId(0x00, this) after detecting the ready-to-U8P_GUARDEGG::hatch state to clear the egg id.
Secondary conditional gated by global flag guard1met: if not set, set xavierSitting and start Item::use; otherwise skip these side effects.
Item::use is invoked as a spawned/child process and its return is captured into local 'temp'.
open_questions: What is stored at stack/local slot SP-00h whose address is passed to Item::use (likely an item/context pointer)?
What exact gameplay effect does Item::use perform here (spawn a creature, grant an item, play animation)? This is inferred but not explicit.
What broader purpose does the xavierSitting flag serve in game logic and which NPC/state transitions it controls?
Is guard1met intended as a one-time guard for player progression (prevents repeated side effects) or does it represent a different condition?

main_function.name: U8P_GUARDEGG::hatch
main_function.purpose: Handle an egg instance finishing incubation: detect ready-to-U8P_GUARDEGG::hatch state, clear the egg id, and optionally trigger NPC state change and an item-use process to realize the U8P_GUARDEGG::hatch outcome.
entry_conditions: Invoked by the engine as a process (processType 0x0210) for the item/egg instance ('this').
Executes its U8P_GUARDEGG::hatch behavior when Egg::getEggId(this) == 0x01 (ready-to-U8P_GUARDEGG::hatch).

function_map.name: U8P_GUARDEGG::hatch
function_map.role: egg hatching handler: detect ready state, reset egg id, set side-effect flags, and start item-use process

function_map.name: Egg::getEggId
function_map.role: query egg instance id/state to determine readiness to U8P_GUARDEGG::hatch

function_map.name: Egg::setEggId
function_map.role: clear or change egg id/state after U8P_GUARDEGG::hatch

function_map.name: Item::use
function_map.role: perform the item-use behavior invoked after U8P_GUARDEGG::hatch (likely spawn/effect); called as a process

external_calls.purpose: Check the current egg id/state to determine if the egg is ready to U8P_GUARDEGG::hatch (compare to 0x01).
Reset the egg id/state to 0 (0x00) after U8P_GUARDEGG::hatch to mark the egg as no longer active/ready.
Invoke the item-use process associated with the U8P_GUARDEGG::hatch (argument passed by address from SP-00h); likely spawns or applies the hatched item's effect and returns a result stored in 'temp'.

reads.name: guard1met
reads.likely_effect: Gates one-time or conditional side effects (if set, skip setting xavierSitting and calling Item::use).

writes.name: xavierSitting
writes.likely_effect: Set to true when a U8P_GUARDEGG::hatch occurs (if guard1met is not set); likely marks an NPC (Xavier) as sitting/changed state in response to the U8P_GUARDEGG::hatch.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: U8P_GUARDEGG::hatch runs as a process to handle an egg reaching a ready-to-U8P_GUARDEGG::hatch state.
